ddls:
CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "plot_name" TEXT /* Name of the plot associated with the event */,
  "alarm_type" TEXT /* Type of alarm set for this event */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the event */,
  "output_value" TEXT /* Output value calculated for this event */,
  "limits" TEXT /* Limits defined for the output value */,
  "event_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the event occurred */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., system start, system stop, error) */,
  "source_system" TEXT /* Name of the system or component generating the event */,
  "event_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the event */,
  "event_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the event (e.g., info, warning, critical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alarms" (
  "alarm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alarm */,
  "alarm_name" TEXT /* Name of the alarm */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alarm's purpose */,
  "threshold_value" REAL /* Threshold value at which the alarm triggers */,
  "alarm_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alarm (e.g., info, warning, critical) */,
  "alarm_duration" INTEGER /* Duration for which the alarm should remain active */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Custom message to be sent when the alarm triggers */,
  "notification_channels" TEXT /* Channels through which notifications should be sent (e.g., email, SMS, Slack) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alarm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "status_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status log entry */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event associated with the log */,
  "status" TEXT /* New status of the event */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the status was updated */,
  "previous_status" TEXT /* Previous status of the event before the update */,
  "status_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the status change */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the status change (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_status_logs_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_status_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "outputs" (
  "output_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each output entry */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event associated with the output */,
  "output_value" TEXT /* Calculated output value */,
  "calculation_date" TEXT /* Date when the output value was calculated */,
  "output_type" TEXT /* Type of output value (e.g., rate, timing, value) */,
  "output_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the output value (e.g., seconds, percentage) */,
  "output_source" TEXT /* Source of the output value (e.g., sensor, log) */,
  "output_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the output value was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("output_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_outputs_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "limit_definitions" (
  "limit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each limit definition */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event associated with the limits */,
  "lower_limit" REAL /* Lower limit for the output */,
  "upper_limit" REAL /* Upper limit for the output */,
  "limit_type" TEXT /* Type of limit (e.g., hard, soft) */,
  "limit_description" TEXT /* Description of the limit */,
  "limit_unit" TEXT /* Unit of the limit (e.g., seconds, percentage) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("limit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_limit_definitions_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for accessing the platform */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
